75.reason: The insertion logic is incorrect; it does not handle the case where the new edge should be inserted before the first element (when r->adj > ver2). This can cause edges to be inserted in the wrong order or lost.

166.reason: Error: Potential null pointer dereference if 'p' is NULL. Additionally, the deletion logic starts checking from the second node, failing to remove the edge if it is the first one in the adjacency list.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [This assumes p is not NULL (head exists) and does not check if the head itself should be removed. Also, if head is NULL, accessing p->elink is illegal.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [Assumes p is not NULL; if vertex i has no edges (possible after deletion), this dereferences NULL pointer]

167.code: for (; r != NULL; r = r->elink, p = p->elink){
167.reason: [The loop increments p and r simultaneously, so p never points to the node before r. Consequently, when r->adj == ver, we cannot link the previous node to r->next, making removal incorrect. Also, the head node (p) is never checked for removal.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  [The loop increments p and r simultaneously, so p never points to the node before r.
